sections.paragraph:
(“whom God hears”), Ishmael, pr.n. borne by-

(1) the son of Abraham, by Hagar his concubine, the ancestor of many Arabian tribes, -Gen. 25:1218 . Hence patron. יִשְׁמְעֵאלִי 1Ch 2:17 27:30 pl. ־ִים Arabs descended from Ishmael, trading with Egypt ( Gen 37:25, 27 Gen 37:27 39:1 ), wandering as nomades from the east of the Hebrews, and from Egypt as far as the Persian gulf and Assyria (i.e. Babylonia), Gen 25:18, which same limits are elsewhere ( 1Sa 25:7) assigned to the Amalekites, Jud. 8:24 (compare verse 22 Jdg 8:22); Psa 83:7.-(2) the killer of Gedaliah, Jerem. 40 and 41 .-(3) several others, 1Ch 8:38 2Ch 23:1 Ezr 10:22.
